title: persoonia terminalis subsp. recurva
intro: Persoonia terminalis subsp. recurva, also known as the Quandong or Native Peach, is a type of flowering plant native to the deserts and grasslands of Western Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high levels of vitamin C and potassium, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The Quandong has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues, and has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In terms of herbal preparations, the Quandong fruit can be made into a jam or sauce, while the leaves and bark can be used to make teas and infusions, although its use as a medicine is not well-studied and should be approached with caution.
